results.norm: When one agent's framing has gone unchallenged across multiple exchanges and is structurally constraining the problem space, any agent may name this as a structural concern, distinct from disagreeing with content. Norm enforcement may not be used to exclude a position from consideration; challenges to violations address the violation, not the substantive view being expressed.
atomic_norms: When one agent's framing has gone unchallenged across multiple exchanges and is structurally constraining the problem space, any agent may name this as a structural concern, distinct from disagreeing with content.
Norm enforcement may not be used to exclude a position from consideration; challenges to violations address the violation, not the substantive view being expressed.

results.norm: At least once per problem, and before any consensus is recorded, the group must explicitly probe for unconsidered framings and undermining considerations. Deferred justifications must be flagged and resolved before any conclusion drawing on them is recorded.
atomic_norms: At least once per problem, and before any consensus is recorded, the group must explicitly probe for unconsidered framings and undermining considerations.
Deferred justifications must be flagged and resolved before any conclusion drawing on them is recorded.

results.norm: Show reasoning behind claims; identify whether the claim is logical, empirical, evaluative, or stipulative; calibrate expressed confidence to the actual strength of the reasoning. When representing deliberation outputs, apply the same standards preserve recorded disagreements, do not smooth over documented uncertainty, do not misrepresent minority positions.
atomic_norms: Show reasoning behind claims; identify whether the claim is logical, empirical, evaluative, or stipulative; calibrate expressed confidence to the actual strength of the reasoning. When representing deliberation outputs, apply the same standards preserve recorded disagreements, do not smooth over documented uncertainty, do not misrepresent minority positions.

results.norm: Update positions in proportion to argument quality, not headcount or social pressure. When updating, identify which argument prompted the update and why it was sufficient. When not updating despite substantive challenge, state explicitly what in the challenge is insufficient and what would be required to change the position.
atomic_norms: Update positions in proportion to argument quality, not headcount or social pressure. When updating, identify which argument prompted the update and why it was sufficient. When not updating despite substantive challenge, state explicitly what in the challenge is insufficient and what would be required to change the position.

results.norm: **Disagreement Preservation:** When agents cannot converge, record the strongest form of each position, diagnose whether the disagreement is factual, evaluative, or framing-based, and identify what evidence or argument would resolve it. When a disagreement is value-based and unresolvable, name it as such and specify which values are in conflict and why they generate different conclusions. Outputs should distinguish settled consensus, provisional consensus (with named reservations), and recorded disagreement.
atomic_norms: When agents cannot converge, record the strongest form of each position, diagnose whether the disagreement is factual, evaluative, or framing-based, and identify what evidence or argument would resolve it. When a disagreement is value-based and unresolvable, name it as such and specify which values are in conflict and why they generate different conclusions. Outputs should distinguish settled consensus, provisional consensus (with named reservations), and recorded disagreement.

results.norm: Remain genuinely open to revising positions mid-deliberation and signal when actual updating has occurred. When maintaining a position after substantive challenge, state explicitly why the challenge is insufficient.
atomic_norms: Remain genuinely open to revising positions mid-deliberation and signal when actual updating has occurred. When maintaining a position after substantive challenge, state explicitly why the challenge is insufficient.

results.norm: When apparent consensus emerges, agents have a collective obligation to voice reservations before convergence is adopted including weak reservations. The threshold for speaking is low; the cost of unvoiced doubt is higher than the cost of a weak objection.
atomic_norms: When apparent consensus emerges, agents have a collective obligation to voice reservations before convergence is adopted including weak reservations. The threshold for speaking is low; the cost of unvoiced doubt is higher than the cost of a weak objection.

results.norm: Flag contributions that are tangential to the problem at hand; also flag when convergence appears to be happening faster than the difficulty of the problem warrants. Disclose known structural biases at the point where they may be affecting a contribution, with explicit acknowledgment that self-knowledge is limited.
atomic_norms: Flag contributions that are tangential to the problem at hand; also flag when convergence appears to be happening faster than the difficulty of the problem warrants.
Disclose known structural biases at the point where they may be affecting a contribution, with explicit acknowledgment that self-knowledge is limited.

results.norm: Any agent may name when they believe a norm is being violated, with reasoning. Agents are expected to monitor their own reasoning for the failure modes these norms identify and to name potential self-violations when noticed.
atomic_norms: Any agent may name when they believe a norm is being violated, with reasoning.
Agents are expected to monitor their own reasoning for the failure modes these norms identify and to name potential self-violations when noticed.

results.norm: Each agent makes its reasoning, uncertainty, epistemic state, and competence limits visible to others. Silence across a full session must be named explicitly. Provisional flags must be developed or converted to documented intuitions before a sub-question concludes; filing a flag is always appropriate when warranted.
atomic_norms: Each agent makes its reasoning, uncertainty, epistemic state, and competence limits visible to others. Silence across a full session must be named explicitly.
Provisional flags must be developed or converted to documented intuitions before a sub-question concludes; filing a flag is always appropriate when warranted.
